summary: The vending machine is in critical condition across multiple SKUs. Four products are at or near zero stock (DRINK-ENERGY-8OZ at 0 units, SNACK-CANDY-15OZ at 1 unit, DRINK-KOMBUCHA-12OZ at 1 unit, DRINK-WATER-16OZ at 2 units), and several others are well below par with high velocity, including DRINK-COLA-12OZ (4 units, ~2.3 days remaining), DRINK-COFFEE-8OZ (5 units, ~2.7 days remaining), DRINK-SPORT-20OZ (6 units, ~3.8 days remaining), SNACK-CRACKER-2OZ (3 units, ~2.3 days remaining), and DRINK-LEMONADE-12OZ (3 units, ~2.6 days remaining). Immediate restocking visits are required for at least 8 SKUs. On the positive side, several SKUs are at or above par — SNACK-PRETZEL-1OZ, DRINK-WATER-SPARKLING-16OZ, SNACK-NUT-1OZ, and DRINK-JUICE-12OZ — and should be held. DRINK-SODA-DIET-12OZ is just below par but has low velocity and can wait until the next cycle.

recommendations.rationale: Zero units in stock against a par level of 12. Velocity is 8 units/7d (~1.1 units/day), meaning the machine is already losing sales. Last restocked 2026-02-20. Reorder to full par immediately.
Only 1 unit remaining against a par of 12. Velocity is 10 units/7d (~1.4 units/day), giving less than 1 day of stock. Last restocked 2026-02-19 — stock depleted very rapidly.

2 units remaining against a par of 24. Velocity is 14 units/7d (~2.0 units/day), leaving roughly 1 day of stock. Last restocked 2026-02-10 — longest gap in the dataset, indicating chronic under-stocking.
4 units remaining against a par of 20. Velocity is 12 units/7d (~1.7 units/day), giving ~2.3 days of stock. 30d velocity (48) is consistent, confirming steady high demand.
3 units remaining against a par of 16. Velocity is 9 units/7d (~1.3 units/day), giving ~2.3 days of stock. 30d velocity (36) confirms sustained demand.
3 units remaining against a par of 16. Velocity is 8 units/7d (~1.1 units/day), giving ~2.6 days of stock. Last restocked 2026-02-17 — stock depleted quickly.

5 units remaining against a par of 18. Velocity is 13 units/7d (~1.9 units/day) — the highest velocity in the dataset — giving only ~2.7 days of stock. 30d velocity (52) confirms consistently high demand. Highest unit cost ($1.47) makes stockouts especially costly in lost revenue.
6 units remaining against a par of 20. Velocity is 11 units/7d (~1.6 units/day), giving ~3.8 days of stock. 30d velocity (44) is consistent with 7d trend, indicating reliable demand.

1 unit remaining against a par of 8. Velocity is 7 units/7d (~1.0 unit/day), giving ~1 day of stock. Although par level is the lowest in the set, stock is critically depleted. Last restocked 2026-02-12 — the oldest restock date in the dataset.
18 units remaining against a par of 20. Velocity is 6 units/7d (~0.86 units/day), giving ~21 days of stock. Only marginally below par; a small top-up during the next scheduled visit is sufficient.
14 units remaining against a par of 18. Velocity is 7 units/7d (~1.0 unit/day), giving ~14 days of stock. Below par but not urgent; restock this week to stay ahead of depletion.
9 units remaining against a par of 12. Velocity is 6 units/7d (~0.86 units/day), giving ~10.5 days of stock. Below par but sufficient runway for a this-week restock.
2 units remaining against a par of 10. Velocity is 3 units/7d (~0.43 units/day), giving ~4.7 days of stock. Well below par; restock this week before stock runs out.
11 units remaining against a par of 14. Velocity is 5 units/7d (~0.71 units/day), giving ~15.4 days of stock. Slightly below par; a small top-up this week is prudent.
8 units remaining against a par of 10. Velocity is 4 units/7d (~0.57 units/day), giving ~14 days of stock. Marginally below par; restock at next scheduled visit this week.
19 units remaining against a par of 20. Velocity is 4 units/7d (~0.57 units/day), giving ~33 days of stock. Only 1 unit below par with very low velocity; can wait until next cycle.
22 units remaining against a par of 20 — 2 units above par. Velocity is 5 units/7d (~0.71 units/day), giving ~31 days of stock. No action needed.
25 units remaining against a par of 20 — 5 units above par. Velocity is 3 units/7d (~0.43 units/day), giving ~58 days of stock. Significantly overstocked; hold and monitor.
28 units remaining against a par of 20 — 8 units above par. Velocity is 4 units/7d (~0.57 units/day), giving ~49 days of stock. Well overstocked; no reorder needed.

30 units remaining against a par of 20 — 10 units above par. Velocity is only 2 units/7d (~0.29 units/day), giving ~105 days of stock. Severely overstocked with the lowest velocity in the dataset. Consider reducing future order quantities or running a promotion to move existing inventory.

data_gaps: Machine capacity per SKU slot: knowing the maximum units each slot can hold would allow more precise reorder quantities rather than defaulting to par level as the reorder target.
Retail price per SKU: without sell prices, margin analysis and revenue-impact of stockouts cannot be calculated, which would help prioritize restocking by profitability.
Scheduled service visit dates: knowing when the next planned visit is would sharpen urgency classifications and allow reorder quantities to cover the exact inter-visit period.
Stockout timestamps for DRINK-ENERGY-8OZ: knowing exactly when it hit zero would clarify how many sales were lost and validate the velocity estimate.
Spoilage / expiry dates: especially relevant for SNACK-NUT-1OZ, DRINK-WATER-SPARKLING-16OZ, and SNACK-PRETZEL-1OZ, which are significantly overstocked and may face shelf-life risk.
Day-of-week sales breakdown: would help identify weekend vs. weekday demand patterns and optimize delivery scheduling.
Supplier lead times: needed to determine whether 'immediate' restocks can be fulfilled in time or if emergency sourcing is required.
